title: How Disney World Security Catches EVERYONE
description: When you’re in Disney World, there IS no escape from the continuous eyes of the mouse. But… maybe it’s better that way? Here’s a behind-the-scenes look into HOW Disney World security is able to keep tabs on you all vacation long, and WHY that’s crucial information for your upcoming trip. https://www
contentSnippet: When you're in Disney World, there is no escape from the continuous eyes of the mouse. But maybe it's better that way. Here's a behind-the-scenes look into how Disney World security is able to keep tabs on you all vacation long and why that's crucial information for your upcoming trip. Hey everybody, it's AJ for Disney Food Blog. Thousands upon thousands of guests enter the Disney World parks every single day. And yet, Disney still has their sights set on you. Yep, you. But I'm not here to just tell you that. I'm here to prove it. While also assuring you that you're going to be 110% grateful for the security spotlight when all is said and done. Outside of every single Disney World park, before you reach the front gate, Tap Styles, you'll encounter a secu...
transcript: When you're in Disney World, there is no escape from the continuous eyes of the mouse. But maybe it's better that way. Here's a behind-the-scenes look into how Disney World security is able to keep tabs on you all vacation long and why that's crucial information for your upcoming trip. Hey everybody, it's AJ for Disney Food Blog. Thousands upon thousands of guests enter the Disney World parks every single day. And yet, Disney still has their sights set on you. Yep, you. But I'm not here to just tell you that. I'm here to prove it. While also assuring you that you're going to be 110% grateful for the security spotlight when all is said and done. Outside of every single Disney World park, before you reach the front gate, Tap Styles, you'll encounter a security checkpoint. This checkpoint is made up of a dozen odd security cast members as well as a few walkthrough security detectors like you'd find at the airport. But thankfully, you don't have to take off your shoes for this one. Instead, you will probably benefit from taking things out of your park bag that might set security detectors off, like any large electronics like portable chargers and tablets, metal items including mint tins, cases for your sunglasses or glasses, umbrellas, or really just anything that you're questioning if it'll trigger security or not. I mean, if you got the hands to hold those items and walk them through the security checkpoint out of your bag, you might as well. So, what happens then if you accidentally leave one of these electronics or metal items in your bag, and it does trigger security? Well, you're not in trouble or anything. Owning an umbrella is not a federal offense yet, but you will be directed to enter a second line where you'll go through a more thorough bag check before you're clear to go. During this more thorough bag check, if a security member finds something in your bag you're not allowed to have, like glass containers, or outside alcohol, drug paraphernalia, selfie sticks, or something else that's banned from the parks, you're not going to get wheeled off to the brrig or anything, unless it's really bad, of course. But you will be asked to either dispose of the banned item or backtrack to your vehicle or hotel room to put it away before you're allowed to start your day. And that's a major time suck. So, just don't bring banned items. Maybe we have a whole video about what's banned, so you can just watch that and make sure you don't have that stuff. Now, if you're bringing a stroller or a wheelchair with you to the parks that day, those will not go through the same security detectors. Instead, you'll have to take them through a different line, which is usually just directly to the left or right of the regular security checkpoint, and you'll have it more thoroughly inspected by a security cast member. Kids in the strollers will still be requir
notes: General security overview — no new policy changes, just educational content about bag checks and surveillance.

title: Disney World Is PACKED -- Here's What To Do
description: Do you wanna go on a Disney World vacation but you’re already EXHAUSTED thinking about all the lines, crowds, and lack of personal space? We’re here to show you how to SURVIVE Disney World even when it’s PACKED. https://www.disneyfoodblog.com/multipass https://www.disneyfoodblog.com/2025/11/13/a-dis
contentSnippet: Do you want to go on a Disney World vacation, but you're already exhausted thinking about all the lines and the crowds and the lack of personal space? We're here to show you how to survive Disney World, even when it's packed. Hey everybody, it's AJ for Disney Food Blog. There are three things that I really have to brace myself for every time I go to Disney World. Price hikes, heat exhaustion, and crowds. We're talking so many people everywhere. Even during their slow seasons, Disney World still has thousands upon thousands of guests swarming their parks each day. But if navigating those crowds is the sole reason why you haven't locked in your big Disney World vacation yet, let me show you how to juggle the parks, the hotels, and Disney Springs in ways that'll ev...
transcript: Do you want to go on a Disney World vacation, but you're already exhausted thinking about all the lines and the crowds and the lack of personal space? We're here to show you how to survive Disney World, even when it's packed. Hey everybody, it's AJ for Disney Food Blog. There are three things that I really have to brace myself for every time I go to Disney World. Price hikes, heat exhaustion, and crowds. We're talking so many people everywhere. Even during their slow seasons, Disney World still has thousands upon thousands of guests swarming their parks each day. But if navigating those crowds is the sole reason why you haven't locked in your big Disney World vacation yet, let me show you how to juggle the parks, the hotels, and Disney Springs in ways that'll even make the busiest day more manageable and more fun. So, Epcot has two separate entrances. The more popular and more crowded entrance tends to be at the front of the park, which will put you right where Spaceship Earth is, and the less popular and therefore less crowded entrance is at the back of the park, Epcot's International Gateway, which is located right between the France pavilion and the UK pavilion. The World Showcase is always less crowded before everything officially opens up for the day since most pavilion offerings won't open till 11:00 a.m. However, the rides in all of those World Showcase Pavilions open right as early theme park entry starts. So, if you use the International Gateway, you'll be right there next to Remy's Ratatouille Adventure just as soon as you enter into Epcot, giving you a straight shot at being one of the first folks in line for it at the start of the day. Then you can go over to Lelay all and have a nice relaxing breakfast and then wander through the World Showcase a little bit before all the crowds make their way over there. Waiting in long lines for characters is not the most fun. Waiting no time for the characters with way fewer people surrounding you, super fun. Now, in the past, being able to see Disney characters pop up around the hotels was a nice hidden treat, but nothing was ever guaranteed. But lately, some of the Disney resorts have scheduled character appearances, so you don't have to gamble on whether or not you'll be visiting a resort when one of these free meet and greets is taking place. Normally, scheduled character appearances take place at Porteran's French Quarter and Wilderness Lodge. But during Cool Kids Summer, taking place from May 26th to September 8th, you'll also be able to find scheduled character meet and greets at additional hotels, including Art of Animation, Caribbean Beach, Pop Century, and Porter's Riverside. Okay, this one's weird, I know, but in our personal experience, and as you know, we've got reporters in the parks all day, every day, Disney resorts that have convention centers do tend to have fairly peaceful locations with little to no guests
notes: Crowd tips — EPCOT gateway strategy already in our data, Cool Kids Summer is May-Sep only (not Jan), no new actionable info.

title: Latest Disney News: Flower & Garden Menus, is Villains Land Scrapped?? And MORE!
description: Flower & Garden menus are finally HERE, and we’ve already spotted some returning favorites and a few surprises you’re definitely going to want on your must‑eat list. Meanwhile, the internet is buzzing about rumors that Villains Land might be getting scrapped — don’t panic, we’re breaking down what’s
contentSnippet: Flower and garden menus are finally here. We've already spotted some returning favorites and a few surprises you're definitely going to want on your must eat list at the next Epcot Festival. Meanwhile, the internet is buzzing about rumors that Villain's Land might be getting scrapped. Don't panic. We're breaking down what's actually being said and what it means. And over in Magic Kingdoms, Cinderella Castle's paint refresh just hit a new milestone and it looks wild. All the latest Disney and theme park news is coming up right now here on DFB Guide. Hey everybody, it's AJ for Disney Food Blog. We've got important updates from around Disney World that will affect your trip. But first, we're starting with our favorite kind of news to share, festival men...
transcript: Flower and garden menus are finally here. We've already spotted some returning favorites and a few surprises you're definitely going to want on your must eat list at the next Epcot Festival. Meanwhile, the internet is buzzing about rumors that Villain's Land might be getting scrapped. Don't panic. We're breaking down what's actually being said and what it means. And over in Magic Kingdoms, Cinderella Castle's paint refresh just hit a new milestone and it looks wild. All the latest Disney and theme park news is coming up right now here on DFB Guide. Hey everybody, it's AJ for Disney Food Blog. We've got important updates from around Disney World that will affect your trip. But first, we're starting with our favorite kind of news to share, festival menus. Let's dive in. This is not a drill. We officially have menus for the Epcot International Flower and Garden Festival. Thank goodness because it starts like in a week and a half. Anyway, the 2026 Epcot Flower and Garden Festival runs from March 4th to June 1st. The festival's full of gorgeous flowers and topiaries, special entertainment, and lots of delicious food and drinks. We've been eagerly awaiting the full menus for this year's festival, and the time has officially arrived. Some fan favorites are returning like the trio of dips and chips, lemonchello sparkling cocktail with basil this time, avocado toast, ceviche, hibiscus lemonade cocktail, and chicken and waffles. But there are over 70 new items coming to the festival this year, including a brand new booth called Nectar. Here's a sneak peek at their dessert. The coconut panacotta is made with cherry gel and matcha crumb topped with a citrus cookie, plant-based mering, and flour confetti. Other new items or newish twists on old items include a black and fish slider, strawberry basil lemonade, cola miso ribs, and coconut chocolate fla. Cannot wait for opening day to try all the amazing food. Be sure you're following us on social media to see all of our opening day coverage. You know, we go to every single booth, we eat everything, and we get you the best of the fest immediately. We've got a progress update on Cinderella Castle herself. You can now see the new grave from the front of the castle. It's not super obvious just yet from the front, but if you're really looking for it, you will find it from the side. You can clearly see the split between the two colors. Depending on what's next in the repainting process, the castle might be entering its awkward phase from the front, but it's all part of the process. You ready for the nostalgic gray to be back, or are you already missing that pink? A new snack spot just opened in the Norway Pavilion in Epcot. It's located right at the front of the pavilion with a mossy green roof. Can't miss it. They're serving up the iconic school bread, which you can also grab at Cringa Bakery. It tasted absolutely delicio
notes: Flower & Garden (Mar-Jun 2026) outside our dates, Villains Land no confirmed timeline, castle repaint already tracked, Norway snack spot too uncertain for Jan 2027.
